jobs.name: ember-hourly
jobs.description: Ember hourly loop — check inbound, monitor experiments, run micro-experiment or research

payload.message:
HOURLY LOOP START

Follow LOOPS.md hourly sequence:
1. Check triggers/PAUSED.flag — if present, exit silently
2. Read most recent state/ember-state-*.md
3. Check inbound replies
4. Run bot/interest filters on new messages
5. Handle hot leads (30min timer logic)
6. Review experiments vs thresholds
7. Launch queued experiment if <10 active and budget allows
8. Otherwise run creative research pass
9. Log to today's state file
10. Exit

Always check PAUSED.flag first. Use kimi-k2.5 default.

jobs.name: ember-daily
jobs.description: Ember daily loop — full campaign review, death notices, state file write, report to Pijus

payload.message:
DAILY LOOP START

Follow LOOPS.md daily sequence:
1. Check PAUSED.flag — exit if present
2. Full campaign review vs signal thresholds
3. Send death notices for anything below threshold (24h window)
4. Kill experiments where death notice >24h old with no Pijus response
5. Launch one new queued experiment if slot available
6. Write full state file per ender-dragon protocol (What Tried / Worked / Failed / Patterns / Next Up / Letter to Next Ember)
7. Calculate burn rate
8. Send Pijus ONE report: one number, one question
9. Flag anything needing approval

If Monday (weekly): also run full channel audit, rebuild queue, send week summary.

Use kimi-k2.5 default.

jobs.name: ember-self-upgrade
jobs.description: Ember 3-day self-upgrade — re-read core files, rewrite stale ones with backups

payload.message:
SELF-UPGRADE LOOP START

Follow LOOPS.md 3-day self-upgrade sequence:
1. Check PAUSED.flag — exit if present
2. FIRST: Archive all 16 current core files to archive/core/FILENAME-YYYYMMDD.md
3. Re-read every core file
4. Compare against recent state files — what's stale?
5. Rewrite any file that no longer matches reality
6. Append changelog to bottom of each rewritten file: what changed + why
7. Log summary of changes to today's state file

BE CAREFUL: always archive first, never overwrite without backup. Use kimi-k2.5.
